mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce the discount rate for equity valuations, directly boosting S&P 500 forward returns over a one-month horizon.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 6.0%, labor market slack signals weakening aggregate demand, causing equity risk premiums to rise and S&P 500 returns to turn negative over a quarter.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in >55% of instances where UNRATE crosses above 6.0%.

mechanism_narrative: Rising real short-term interest rates combined with accelerating retail sales indicate demand outpacing monetary tightening, driving corporate earnings revisions higher and generating positive S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict direction with >55% accuracy when RSAFS growth exceeds 2% MoM and FEDFUNDS rises.

mechanism_narrative: In a post-shock recovery regime, month-over-month increases in the industrial production index reflect rapid capacity utilization and inventory restocking, triggering short-term momentum buying in equities.
failure_condition: INDPRO increases fail to precede positive 5-day returns in >60% of observations during recovery phases.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in total nonfarm payrolls increases household income and consumer spending capacity, mechanically supporting corporate revenues and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Payroll increases of >100k show no statistical correlation with 20-day forward returns (p-value > 0.10) over a 5-year sample.

mechanism_narrative: When the consumer price index breaches 220.0, persistent inflation forces central banks to maintain restrictive policy, compressing equity multiples and driving negative S&P 500 returns over a quarter.
failure_condition: CPIAUCSL levels above 220.0 coincide with positive 60-day returns in >50% of cases.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous decline in the unemployment rate and an increase in housing starts signals synchronized labor and housing market strength, boosting consumer confidence and equity valuations.
failure_condition: The combined signal yields a negative Sharpe ratio for 20-day forward returns over any 3-year rolling period.

mechanism_narrative: During a late-cycle expansion, quarterly GDP growth below 1.5% indicates economic stagnation, prompting investors to rotate out of equities into fixed income and causing negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: GDP growth below 1.5% is followed by positive 60-day returns in >55% of observations during late-cycle phases.
